proposed_from.topic: Transplant of honest/bias-aware regression-discontinuity partial identification (Kolesar-Rothe AER'18 discrete running variable; Armstrong-Kolesar optimal bias-aware honest CIs) into LLM-cascade routing: identify the NET escalation policy value V = E[Y(large)-Y(small)-kappa | boundary] of a cascade that escalates query x to the large model iff a learned calibrated confidence score c-hat(x) >= tau-hat. Non-verbatim witness: (1) tau-hat is chosen cost-optimally on the SAME data (UCCI 2605.18796), a SELECTED cutoff, so pointwise-in-cutoff honest RD CIs lose validity -> need post-selection honest bounds uniform over a specified population cutoff-selection functional tau*(P); (2) c-hat is a generated/estimated calibrated score (isotonic steps, mass points) endogenous to Y(small) (the small model's own confidence), breaking the classical continuity-of-CEF-in-running-variable proof; (3) deterministic threshold routing fails backdoor-ATE positivity globally (2605.25998 leaves this open), so only the LOCAL net-value at the boundary is identified - a different functional. Target the honest identified set [L*(M),U*(M)] for the POPULATION net value of the selected routing rule under bounded calibration-CEF curvature M, with critical curvature/cost-slack M* at which 0 enters [L*,U*] (sign of net escalation value loses identification), plus the post-selection-honest estimation rung (bias-aware local-linear endpoint estimator with uniform coverage over the M-class and the cutoff-selection; generated-regressor correction). Target tier: field.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"There is no prior pid_cascade_escalation_rd proposal in the repository. The closest in-repo objects are pid_rdd_manipulation_bounded, which studies endpoint kinks under an envelope-derived manipulation budget but not a selected routing cutoff, and pid_offline_policy_joint_misspec, which contributes selected-target bookkeeping and active-set switching logic in an offline-policy sensitivity problem rather than a discrete-score honest endpoint problem. The chosen kernel reuses those ideas but changes both the target and the geometry: tau_*(P) is a policy-selected cutoff, and the object of interest is the selected boundary value under score-support curvature restrictions.","published_axis":"The closest published results split into four non-overlapping pieces: UCCI chooses the routing threshold from the right application but offers no honest selected-target value theory; Armstrong-Kolesar and Kolesar-Rothe solve fixed-cutoff honest endpoint problems; Armstrong-Kolesar bandwidth snooping solves selected-reporting for fixed targets; Bachoc-Preinerstorfer-Steinberger solves selected-target coverage in parametric model families. No cited paper composes those elements into a sharp selected-cutoff identified set for a population routing threshold, nor into a same-sample generated-score honest CI for that target."}
seed_list: Selected-cutoff honest identified set: define tau_*(P) from the population cost-quality frontier and derive the sharp M-curvature interval [L_*(P,M), U_*(P,M)] for V_* = E[Y_l - Y_s - kappa | C = tau_*(P)].
Critical-curvature frontier: characterize M_*(P) = inf{M : 0 in [L_*(P,M), U_*(P,M)]} and the active support constraints that trigger sign loss.
Generated-score honest RD with isotonic mass points: define c_P and add a first-stage correction so selected-cutoff endpoint inference remains valid when C is estimated.
Local-vs-global routing value decomposition: separate the identified boundary gain from the unidentified global policy value under deterministic threshold routing.
Post-selection honest CI for same-sample threshold choice: adapt bias-aware local-linear RD plus snooping critical values to a selected policy cutoff.
Non-LP discrete-score geometry: derive closed-form or support-function bounds when isotonic calibration creates finitely many score plateaus around tau_*(P).
Tie-robust selected-target inference: directional-differentiability or union-over-near-optimizers for flat population frontiers in isotonic routing.
Cost-slack sensitivity frontier: add a calibration or invariance slack delta and define the critical delta_* where net escalation sign is no longer certified.
proposed_from.last_draft_handoff: {"status":"completed","message":"Patched the flagged items in place: rewrote the primitive target as the set-valued selected endpoint functional [L_*(P,M), U_*(P,M)], separated the dense-regime point target V_cont(P) for the open inference handle, retagged the three flagged assumptions as proposal-specific regularity conditions with calibrated-cascade plausibility justifications, and propagated the semantic changes through statements, prose, and sampling model. seed_details.one_line: Selected-cutoff honest identified set: define tau_*(P) from the population cost-quality frontier and derive the sharp M-curvature interval [L_*(P,M), U_*(P,M)] for V_* = E[Y_l - Y_s - kappa | C = tau_*(P)].
seed_details.anchor_open_problem: Post-selection honest partial-identification bounds are missing for the population net value of a same-sample, cost-selected LLM-cascade cutoff.
seed_details.closest_gap: Closest published pieces are fixed-target honest endpoint theory and bandwidth-snooping corrections; neither treats a threshold chosen by the same population policy functional that defines the target.
seed_details.referee_objection: Immediate from Armstrong-Kolesar plus bandwidth snooping because the selected cutoff is just another tuning parameter.
seed_details.rebuttal: Those papers keep the estimand fixed while tuning changes the estimator; here the target itself is tau_*(P)-selected, so target bookkeeping and sharp endpoint geometry must be re-done jointly.

seed_details.one_line: Critical-curvature frontier: characterize M_*(P) = inf{M : 0 in [L_*(P,M), U_*(P,M)]} and the active support constraints that trigger sign loss.
seed_details.anchor_open_problem: The decision-facing critical curvature or cost-slack threshold M* is not characterized for when zero first enters the honest identified set of the selected cascade boundary value.
seed_details.closest_gap: Closest published work widens honest intervals with smoothness or studies cascade frontiers, but does not isolate the first curvature value where local escalation sign ceases to be identified.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just inverting an honest interval in M.
seed_details.rebuttal: With a selected cutoff and discrete isotonic score support, the endpoint geometry can kink when binding support constraints switch, so M_*(P) is not a tautological re-labeling of interval width.

seed_details.one_line: Generated-score honest RD with isotonic mass points: define c_P and add a first-stage correction so selected-cutoff endpoint inference remains valid when C is estimated.
seed_details.anchor_open_problem: Generated calibrated confidence scores in LLM routing lack an honest RD theory when the running variable is estimated, isotonic, mass-pointed, and endogenous to the small model's output.
seed_details.closest_gap: Closest comparators handle fixed discrete running variables or proxy-score designs, not same-sample selected thresholds with isotonic first-stage score estimation.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is a routine DML generated-regressor correction.
seed_details.rebuttal: The missing term is boundary-local and support-sensitive because isotonic calibration creates atoms and flat score plateaus; standard orthogonal-score templates do not deliver the selected endpoint expansion directly.

seed_details.one_line: Local-vs-global routing value decomposition: separate the identified boundary gain from the unidentified global policy value under deterministic threshold routing.
seed_details.anchor_open_problem: Deterministic threshold LLM routing needs a formal local boundary estimand because global backdoor ATE positivity fails away from the selected cutoff.
seed_details.closest_gap: Closest policy-learning RD papers decompose utility under multiple cutoffs; the single-cutoff LLM routing case still lacks a clean local-vs-global estimand split.
seed_details.referee_objection: Safe policy learning under RD already decomposes identified and unidentified utility.
seed_details.rebuttal: That paper uses multiple cutoffs and extrapolation across groups; the single selected LLM routing threshold has a different target, different support failure, and a different selected-cutoff consumer.

seed_details.one_line: Post-selection honest CI for same-sample threshold choice: adapt bias-aware local-linear RD plus snooping critical values to a selected policy cutoff.
seed_details.anchor_open_problem: A post-selection-honest estimator is missing for bias-aware local-linear endpoint inference with uniform coverage over curvature, selected cutoff, generated score, and discrete mass points.
seed_details.closest_gap: Closest published inference papers each solve one piece only: fixed-cutoff RBC, fixed-target snooping, or discrete-score honest RD.
seed_details.referee_objection: Just stack the existing corrections and the problem is solved.
seed_details.rebuttal: Stacking is exactly what fails because the selected target, simultaneous threshold search, and generated score interact at first order; the report object has to be defined as one theorem, not as a list of patches.

seed_details.one_line: Non-LP discrete-score geometry: derive closed-form or support-function bounds when isotonic calibration creates finitely many score plateaus around tau_*(P).
seed_details.anchor_open_problem: Generated calibrated confidence scores in LLM routing lack an honest RD theory when the running variable is estimated, isotonic, mass-pointed, and endogenous to the small model's output.
seed_details.closest_gap: Closest work identifies honest fixed-cutoff intervals with discrete support, but not a closed-form selected-target support-function representation.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is a computational reformulation, not new identification content.
seed_details.rebuttal: It could matter if the active-set map is explicit, but absent the selected-target theorem it is secondary rather than the kernel.

seed_details.one_line: Tie-robust selected-target inference: directional-differentiability or union-over-near-optimizers for flat population frontiers in isotonic routing.
seed_details.anchor_open_problem: Post-selection honest partial-identification bounds are missing for the population net value of a same-sample, cost-selected LLM-cascade cutoff.
seed_details.closest_gap: Closest analogue is selected-target coverage under model selection, but not active-set switching of a nonparametric threshold functional with score atoms.
seed_details.referee_objection: Generic argmin-functional delta method already covers this.
seed_details.rebuttal: Score atoms create flat frontiers and set-valued optimizers, so the smooth unique-argmin template is exactly what breaks.

seed_details.one_line: Cost-slack sensitivity frontier: add a calibration or invariance slack delta and define the critical delta_* where net escalation sign is no longer certified.
seed_details.anchor_open_problem: The decision-facing critical curvature or cost-slack threshold M* is not characterized for when zero first enters the honest identified set of the selected cascade boundary value.
seed_details.closest_gap: Closest papers discuss frontier misspecification qualitatively, not a critical delta_* for selected boundary certification.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just a second sensitivity index next to M_*.
seed_details.rebuttal: It is defensible but less sharp than the curvature object because the first contribution is already the selected-target bound itself.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: thm:selected-sharp-set / main identification kernel
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The sharp interval is conditional on the proposal-specific range-respecting C^2 curvature-budget extension class.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The selected endpoint interval is a field-tier sharp bound under the stated smooth-extension model, not unrestricted observed-law point identification.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: prop:critical-curvature / M_*(P)
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The sign-certification frontier is accepted as a conditional sensitivity object indexed by the imposed curvature budget M.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Stage 0 should derive monotonicity and sign-certification under the M-indexed extension class, not treat M_*(P) as an externally validated physical threshold.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: conj:postselect-honest-ci / dense inference rung
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The coverage claim is conditional on constructing the data-only generated-score drift envelope hat G_n and selected-threshold critical value in the locally dense regime.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The inference result is an open dense-regime handle for V_cont(P), not a settled theorem for atomic isotonic-score supports.

banked_reason: D0.5 repeatedly failed on selected-cutoff geometry, generated-score drift, and post-selection honest-CI gaps; the last review still required new math or definition repair beyond an in-place D0.R edit.
